[D1] (20:63-20:79) TS377003: Missing errors ErrorA | ErrorB | ErrorC in the expected Effect type. effect(missingEffectError)
  Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Catch all errors with Effect.catchAll"
  Fix 3: "Catch unexpected errors with Effect.catchTag"

[D2] (23:72-23:88) TS377003: Missing errors ErrorA | ErrorC in the expected Effect type. effect(missingEffectError)
  Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Catch unexpected errors with Effect.catchTag"

=== Quick Fix Application Results ===

=== [D1] Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 2: "Catch all errors with Effect.catchAll" ===

--- file:///.src/missingEffectError_tagged.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Data from "effect/Data"
import type * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

class ErrorA extends Data.TaggedClass("ErrorA")<{
  a: 1
}> {}

class ErrorB extends Data.TaggedClass("ErrorB")<{
  a: 2
}> {}

class ErrorC extends Data.TaggedClass("ErrorC")<{
  a: 3
}> {}

declare const effectWithErrors: Effect.Effect<number, ErrorA | ErrorB | ErrorC>

// @ts-expect-error
export const conciseBody: () => Effect.Effect<number> = () => Effect.catchAll(effectWithErrors, () => Effect.dieMessage("TODO: catchAll not implemented"))

// @ts-expect-error
export const conciseBody2: () => Effect.Effect<number, ErrorB> = () => effectWithErrors


=== [D1] Fix 3: "Catch unexpected errors with Effect.catchTag" ===

--- file:///.src/missingEffectError_tagged.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Data from "effect/Data"
import type * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

class ErrorA extends Data.TaggedClass("ErrorA")<{
  a: 1
}> {}

class ErrorB extends Data.TaggedClass("ErrorB")<{
  a: 2
}> {}

class ErrorC extends Data.TaggedClass("ErrorC")<{
  a: 3
}> {}

declare const effectWithErrors: Effect.Effect<number, ErrorA | ErrorB | ErrorC>

// @ts-expect-error
export const conciseBody: () => Effect.Effect<number> = () => Effect.catchTags(effectWithErrors, { "ErrorA": () => Effect.dieMessage("TODO: catchTags() not implemented for ErrorA"), "ErrorB": () => Effect.dieMessage("TODO: catchTags() not implemented for ErrorB"), "ErrorC": () => Effect.dieMessage("TODO: catchTags() not implemented for ErrorC") })

// @ts-expect-error
export const conciseBody2: () => Effect.Effect<number, ErrorB> = () => effectWithErrors


=== [D2] Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 2: "Catch unexpected errors with Effect.catchTag" ===

--- file:///.src/missingEffectError_tagged.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Data from "effect/Data"
import type * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

class ErrorA extends Data.TaggedClass("ErrorA")<{
  a: 1
}> {}

class ErrorB extends Data.TaggedClass("ErrorB")<{
  a: 2
}> {}

class ErrorC extends Data.TaggedClass("ErrorC")<{
  a: 3
}> {}

declare const effectWithErrors: Effect.Effect<number, ErrorA | ErrorB | ErrorC>

// @ts-expect-error
export const conciseBody: () => Effect.Effect<number> = () => effectWithErrors

// @ts-expect-error
export const conciseBody2: () => Effect.Effect<number, ErrorB> = () => Effect.catchTags(effectWithErrors, { "ErrorA": () => Effect.dieMessage("TODO: catchTags() not implemented for ErrorA"), "ErrorC": () => Effect.dieMessage("TODO: catchTags() not implemented for ErrorC") })
